summary: Set 027 Session 4 of 4 (FINAL): Layer 3 Playwright Electron rendering smoke shipped + set closes. Created src/test/playwright/{electronLaunch.ts, treeView.spec.ts} + playwright.config.ts; added @playwright/test ^1.60.0 dev dep + test:playwright npm script. Five scenarios cover fresh-set, mid-session in flight, all-done, cancelled, and force-closed bucketing/badge rendering via aria-label text assertions. Launches Code.exe directly via _electron.launch (bypasses the @vscode/test-electron path that was broken on this Windows host since Session 3). All 5 tests pass in 1m24s on this orchestrator (Windows 11 + VS Code 1.120) — well under the 3-min spec budget. Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4 in 2 sub-rounds ($0.174 + $0.287 = $0.461); returned 2 Major + 5 Minor findings, all addressed: numeric semver sort for cached-binary discovery; separate _electronEnv() preserving Linux GUI session vars; F1 cross-platform palette shortcut; rewritten CHANGELOG entries clarifying ai_router 0.3.1 harness is repo-only (excluded from wheel); tightened cancel test naming; independent best-effort teardown; per-test setTimeout(120_000) overrides removed; change-log cost row backfilled. The full-env fix uncovered a latent runtime bug — VS Code IPC vars (ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE, VSCODE_*) leaking from a parent IDE host would have flipped Code.exe into CLI-arg-parsing mode; added explicit blocklist + VSCODE_ prefix scrub. Versions bumped: ai_router 0.3.0->0.3.1; extension 0.13.15->0.13.16. CHANGELOG entries written for both packages. CLAUDE.md updated with Layer 1/2/3 test commands + layer-choice guidance. change-log.md written for the set; activity-log + state files updated. Set total cost: $1.86 of $5.00 NTE (37% of budget). Release tags pushed by operator post-merge per standard workflow.

reason.specifics: Set 027 closes with Session 4. No follow-up session in this set. The two drift classes pinned by the harness (register_session_start completedSessions[] omission on fresh sets; isMidSetComplete downgrade of force-closed mid-set) are candidate work for a follow-up set if operator decides to fix them — both deserve targeted reader/writer changes with backwards-compat care. No next_orchestrator dependency from this close-out.
